title: image push resumed at wrong offset
body:
I'm seeing sporadic push failures... from the buildah side, the error is:

```
2018-10-28T02:39:21.258666068Z error: build error: Failed to push image: error copying layers and metadata from "containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.skip_mount_home=false,overlay.mountopt=nodev,overlay.override_kernel_check=false]docker-registry.default.svc:5000/e2e-test-build-image-source-r8gd7/inputimage:latest" to "docker://docker-registry.default.svc:5000/e2e-test-build-image-source-r8gd7/inputimage:latest": Error writing blob: Error uploading layer to https://docker-registry.default.svc:5000/v2/e2e-test-build-image-source-r8gd7/inputimage/blobs/uploads/c38c3834-65d9-4378-9b85-d0c6d841abb3?_state=x36zMpkRWAUwQMA45gqwbIwb4oOSQmGRqi8shuHLjM17Ik5hbWUiOiJlMmUtdGVzdC1idWlsZC1pbWFnZS1zb3VyY2UtcjhnZDcvaW5wdXRpbWFnZSIsIlVVSUQiOiJjMzhjMzgzNC02NWQ5LTQzNzgtOWI4NS1kMGM2ZDg0MWFiYjMiLCJPZmZzZXQiOjE3NTE1NSwiU3RhcnRlZEF0IjoiMjAxOC0xMC0yOFQwMjozOTowOVoifQ%3D%3D&digest=sha256%3Aa38d357764a3aa20c2241433436db6cc24ea898a7f3c0904d66ca2323f302123: blob upload invalid
```


from the registry side, the error is:

```
time="2018-10-28T02:39:20.748537421Z" level=error msg="upload resumed at wrong offest: 0 != 175155" go.version=go1.10.3 http.request.contenttype=application/octet-stream http.request.host="docker-registry.default.svc:5000" http.request.id=41427703-4ca2-4743-8a88-681d8dd2d397 http.request.method=PUT http.request.remoteaddr="172.16.2.15:51296" http.request.uri="/v2/e2e-test-build-image-source-r8gd7/inputimage/blobs/uploads/c38c3834-65d9-4378-9b85-d0c6d841abb3?_state=x36zMpkRWAUwQMA45gqwbIwb4oOSQmGRqi8shuHLjM17Ik5hbWUiOiJlMmUtdGVzdC1idWlsZC1pbWFnZS1zb3VyY2UtcjhnZDcvaW5wdXRpbWFnZSIsIlVVSUQiOiJjMzhjMzgzNC02NWQ5LTQzNzgtOWI4NS1kMGM2ZDg0MWFiYjMiLCJPZmZzZXQiOjE3NTE1NSwiU3RhcnRlZEF0IjoiMjAxOC0xMC0yOFQwMjozOTowOVoifQ%3D%3D&digest=sha256%3Aa38d357764a3aa20c2241433436db6cc24ea898a7f3c0904d66ca2323f302123" http.request.useragent=Go-http-client/1.1 openshift.auth.user="system:serviceaccount:e2e-test-build-image-source-r8gd7:builder" openshift.auth.userid=45f9ed87-da5a-11e8-9657-42010a8e0004 vars.name=e2e-test-build-image-source-r8gd7/inputimage vars.uuid=c38c3834-65d9-4378-9b85-d0c6d841abb3
```

You can see the full log (both the build output and registry log) here: https://storage.googleapis.com/origin-ci-test/pr-logs/pull/21372/pull-ci-openshift-origin-master-e2e-gcp-builds/5/build-log.txt

/cc @dmage @legionus as it would be useful for you to take a look at the request flow in the registry log but i don't think this is a registry issue as we've only started seeing this failure since moving to buildah to push the images.

comments.body: @bparees though, it looks like a storage problem on the registry side. The client has successfully uploaded 175155 bytes (using `PATCH /v2/e2e-test-build-image-source-r8gd7/inputimage/blobs/uploads/c38c3834-65d9-4378-9b85-d0c6d841abb3`). But the subsequent PUT request doesn't know anything about these bytes.

I couldn't tell from the log whether the registry was expecting the put to start at 175155 but the client set the offset to zero, or vice versa.

(obviously both should be expecting 175155, but it wasn't clear to me which one was using 0 instead).
@bparees Are you still seeing this issue?
I don't think so.  though i'm also unaware what might have fixed it.
Well reopen if it happens again.

The same issue for me, occasionally instead of always happened.

It is worth mentioning that I used an HAProxy before 3 registries, and when I upload an image, some blobs are processed by registry A, and some other blobs are processed by registry B.
